programmes.show_description: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Strangers: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and the team are called in to investigate the tragic events that unfold after a wedding with a bizarre guest list.
Close to Home: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and the team are baffled when a known petty criminal turns into a killer, and Kelsey receives some bad news.
Generation of Vipers: Lewis and Hathaway are drawn into a world of virtual bullying - but very real murder - when a professor is found dead after a dating video is leaked online.
Game: Crime drama. Throwing himself into work to mask the heartache of Joan leaving, a young Endeavour Morse is quickly consumed by his duties when a research scientist is drowned.
Uneasy Lies the Head: Comedy-drama series. Martin struggles to deal with Louisa's news while trying to treat a headmaster with a strange affliction that affects one of his arms.
Up the Garden Path: The organiser of a Garden Open Day Scheme calls on Rosemary and Laura to save the good name of her village when she discovers that its gardens are blighted.
...They Ask Evans? Part one of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1934 novel. Ex-naval lieutenant Bobby Jones drifts idly through life until he finds a dying man...
...They Ask Evans? The second part of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's novel. The death of Bobby's friend and the local police's obduracy hardens Bobby's resolve.
...They Ask Evans? The concluding part of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1934 novel. Frankie and Bobby approach the heart of a dark conspiracy - and an answer.
4:50 from Paddington: When Elspeth McGillicuddy sees a woman on a train being strangled, the authorities think that she has been dreaming, but Miss Marple knows better...
Dead Letters: Midsomer Barton is celebrating Oak Apple Week when a mother is found drowned - it looks like suicide, but Barnaby investigates and is shaken by memories of his past.
Dirty: The murder of a cleaner whose death occurred several hours after the killer blow landed puts DCI Vera Stanhope on an intriguing trail while piecing his final hours together.
Captivating mystery thriller. Jo and Sam interrogate Donna - who yields a revelation that points fingers at the Benson family again - before a surprise confession rocks the case.
Captivating mystery thriller. As the police search for Declan, a bombshell revelation threatens Jo's and Pat's investigation into Mackie before the murderer is finally revealed.
Canteen Trouble: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
The First of the Queue: The very last episode of the classic British sitcom. When Oliver tells Simon about a bargain in the sales, the pair go to extraordinary lengths to scoop it.
The New Telly: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a bus company. Stan and his family decide to buy a colour TV, but there is a problem with the disposal of their old set.
Carl worries about Donald when he has a funny turn and refuses to go to the hospital. Val accuses Pearl of causing the fire, and the villagers learn about Pollard's plans.
Terry confronts Matthew about the apartment development - warning the businessman he will contest the plans. Marlon is offered a new job, and Carl tells Donald to take it easy.
David puts on a brave face for Gail's sake, but breaks down in tears when she leaves. An old flame tries to get in touch with Audrey, and Paul is fuming to hear of Leanne's plans.
Leanne offers Paul his insurance money but, still bitter about being duped, he gives her an ultimatum. Becky and Jason arrange another date and an upbeat Kirk returns from Cyprus.
Not with Kindness: Feature-length drama. There are threats, deaths, arson and a staff shortage at the police station, but anything has to be better than staying at home for Frost.
Manhunt: Drama series about a policeman in 1960s Yorkshire. Nick is taken hostage by an intruder at the Aidensfield Arms and learns of a local trade in stolen farm machinery.
Bitter Harvest: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Nick is called on to investigate a suspected outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease at a remote farm.
Orchis Fatalis: When one of the world's rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death.
Marlon and Val argue when the chef learns that they didn't win the Pub of the Year competition. Sam offers to rebuild the factory, and Mel asks Ashley for time with Arthur.
Mel gives Greg an ultimatum when he confronts her about Arthur. Marlon prepares to contact the rival pub about the job offer but Donna urges him to reconsider.
Leanne tries to reason with Paul about his threat, but Dan decides to call his bluff. A remorseful David receives a visit from Audrey, and Kirk introduces his girlfriend to Fiz.
Leanne and Dan realise they're up to their necks in trouble as Paul heads off to the police station. Liam spends the night on Carla's sofa and Audrey finally arranges to meet Ted.
Over the Hill: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Nick investigates a series of break-ins that point to someone living rough in the woods.
Bang to Rights: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. A chance happening prompts Nick to reopen an old case.
Vixen's Run: Sir Freddy Butler's death looks like a heart attack, but Barnaby and Jones are brought to the privileged surroundings of Haddington Hall when his will disappears.
Tainted Fruit: Feature-length drama. DCI Barnaby uncovers a seedy world of adultery and deceit within an affluent rural community when the body of an arrogant local beauty is found.
Stranger in the House: Detective drama series. DI Frost exposes a junior officer to danger in his effort to trap a particularly dangerous rapist that is at large on his patch.
